NCT03391466.name: ZUMA-7
NCT03391466.group: 2L R/R LBCL refractory/relapsed <=12 mo, axicabtagene ciloleucel vs SoC (R-chemo + BEAM + ASCT)
NCT03391466.snippet: Source: Locke FL et al. N Engl J Med 2022;386:640-654 (ZUMA-7).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 359 adults with large B-cell lymphoma refractory to first-line chemoimmunotherapy or relapsed within 12 months randomized 1:1 open-label to axicabtagene ciloleucel (axi-cel, n=180) or SoC (2-3 cycles of R-chemo then BEAM + autologous stem-cell transplant, n=179).

evidence.label: Primary - Event-Free Survival
evidence.text: Median event-free survival: axi-cel 8.3 months vs SoC 2.0 months; hazard ratio 0.40 (95% CI 0.31 to 0.51; P<0.001). 24-month EFS rate 41% vs 16%. Primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Secondary - ORR and CR Rate
evidence.text: Objective response rate: axi-cel 83% (149/180) vs SoC 50% (90/179); odds ratio 4.8 (95% CI 3.0-7.7). Complete response 65% vs 32%. Grade 3+ cytokine release syndrome 6%; grade 3+ neurotoxicity 21%; no treatment-related deaths.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - IRT randomization. D2 SOME CONCERNS - open-label by necessity (single infusion vs multi-cycle chemo). D3 LOW - 95% primary follow-up. D4 LOW - central IRC-adjudicated PET-CT response. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Key Inclusion Criteria:~* Histologically proven large B-cell lymphoma (BCL) including the following types defined by World Health Organization (WHO) 2016.~  * Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL) not otherwise specified activated B-cell/ germinal center B-cell (ABC/GCB).~ * High-grade B-cell lymphoma (HGBL) with or without myelocytomatosis oncogene (MYC) and BCL 2 and/or BCL 6 rearrangement.~ * DLBCL arising from follicular lymphoma (FL).~ * T-cell/histiocyte rich large B-cell lymphoma.~ * DLB...

NCT03575351.name: TRANSFORM
NCT03575351.group: 2L R/R LBCL refractory/relapsed <=12 mo, lisocabtagene maraleucel vs SoC
NCT03575351.snippet: Source: Kamdar M et al. Lancet 2022;399:2294-2308 (TRANSFORM).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 184 adults with R/R large B-cell lymphoma within 12 months of first-line therapy randomized 1:1 open-label to lisocabtagene maraleucel (liso-cel, n=92) or SoC (n=92). SoC = 3 cycles salvage + BEAM + ASCT for responders.

evidence.label: Primary - Event-Free Survival
evidence.text: Median event-free survival: liso-cel 10.1 months vs SoC 2.3 months; hazard ratio 0.35 (95% CI 0.23 to 0.53; P<0.0001). Primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Secondary - CR and Safety
evidence.text: Complete response rate: liso-cel 66% (61/92) vs SoC 38% (35/92); odds ratio 3.6 (95% CI 1.9-6.8). Grade 3+ cytokine release syndrome 1%; grade 3+ neurotoxicity 4%; favourable safety vs ZUMA-7.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central randomization. D2 SOME CONCERNS - open-label. D3 LOW - 94% completion. D4 LOW - IRC-adjudicated response. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 75 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~1. Subject is ≥ 18 years and ≤ 75 years of age at the time of signing the informed consent form (ICF).~2. Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group (ECOG) performance status ≤ 1.~3. Histologically proven diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL) NOS (de novo or transformed indolent NHL), high grade B-cell lymphoma with MYC and BCL2 and/or BCL6 rearrangements with DLBCL histology (double/triple-hit lymphoma [DHL/THL]), primary mediastinal (thymic) large B-cell lymphoma (PMBCL), T cell...

NCT03570892.name: BELINDA
NCT03570892.group: 2L R/R LBCL refractory/relapsed <=12 mo, tisagenlecleucel vs SoC (this trial was negative)
NCT03570892.snippet: Source: Bishop MR et al. N Engl J Med 2022;386:629-639 (BELINDA).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 322 adults with R/R aggressive B-cell lymphoma within 12 months of first-line therapy randomized 1:1 open-label to tisagenlecleucel (tisa-cel, n=162) or SoC (n=160). SoC = investigator-choice chemo + ASCT for responders; bridging therapy permitted between enrolment and tisa-cel infusion.

evidence.label: Primary - Event-Free Survival (Trial did NOT meet primary)
evidence.text: Median event-free survival: tisa-cel 3.0 months vs SoC 3.0 months; hazard ratio 1.07 (95% CI 0.82 to 1.40; P=0.61). Trial did NOT meet the primary endpoint. Effect direction inconsistent with ZUMA-7 and TRANSFORM; likely driven by longer manufacturing time and heavier bridging.

evidence.label: Secondary - ORR and Manufacturing Time
evidence.text: Overall response rate: tisa-cel 46% vs SoC 43%. Median time from randomization to tisa-cel infusion was 52 days (vs ~30 days for axi-cel in ZUMA-7). 26% of tisa-cel arm received bridging chemo. Post-hoc subgroup analysis suggested outcomes varied by primary refractory status.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - IRT randomization. D2 SOME CONCERNS - open-label + heavy use of bridging therapy in tisa-cel arm may have confounded comparison. D3 LOW - 96% primary follow-up. D4 LOW - IRC-adjudicated response. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 100 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Histologically confirmed, aggressive B-cell NHL at relapse/progression or PR after front line therapy. Aggressive B-cell NHL is heretofore defined by the following list of subtypes (Swerdlow et al 2016):~  * DLBCL, NOS,~ * FL grade 3B,~ * Primary mediastinal large B cell lymphoma (PMBCL),~ * T cell rich/histiocyte rich large B cell lymphoma (T/HRBCL),~ * DLBCL associated with chronic inflammation,~ * Intravascular large B-cell lymphoma,~ * ALK+ large B-cell lymphoma,~ * B-...

title: RapidMeta Haematology | CAR-T as Second-Line Therapy for R/R Aggressive B-Cell Lymphoma v1.0
h1: CAR-T as Second-Line Therapy \u2014 Patient Summary
